title: Spring Cleaning: The Whole House Rebels

sentences.en: Saturday. Sophia declared today "spring cleaning day," a phrase that turns our entire house into a small, chaotic construction site.
sentences.zh: 星期六。Sophia 宣布今天是「春季大掃除日」，這句話讓我們整個家瞬間變成一個小型、混亂的工地現場。

sentences.en: My first task was the balcony, sweeping away a whole winter's worth of dust, dead leaves, and one mysterious sock nobody claimed.
sentences.zh: 我第一個任務是打掃陽台，掃掉一整個冬天累積的灰塵、枯葉，還有一隻沒有人認領的神秘襪子。

sentences.en: Mom took down every curtain in the living room, insisting they had not been washed since "possibly the last century."
sentences.zh: 媽媽把客廳裡每一片窗簾都拆下來，堅持它們「可能從上個世紀」就沒洗過了。

sentences.en: Dad rearranged the furniture, moving the sofa to a completely new corner, which honestly made the whole room feel different, and slightly smaller.
sentences.zh: 爸爸重新擺放了家具，把沙發搬到一個全新的角落，老實說，這讓整個房間感覺不一樣了，也稍微變小了一點。

sentences.en: William's job was folding the small mat by the front door, a task he somehow managed to make last nearly twenty minutes.
sentences.zh: William 的任務是摺門口那張小地墊，這個任務不知怎麼地被他拖了快二十分鐘。

sentences.en: I found Dad's favorite mug behind the sofa, half full of cold tea that had clearly been forgotten there for several days.
sentences.zh: 我在沙發後面找到爸爸最愛的馬克杯，裡面裝著半杯明顯被遺忘好幾天的冷茶。

sentences.en: Sophia handed me a needle and thread, asking me to sew a loose button back onto Dad's favorite shirt before it fell off completely.
sentences.zh: Sophia 遞給我一根針和線，要我把爸爸最喜歡那件襯衫上快掉的鈕扣縫回去。

sentences.en: I am not particularly skilled with a needle, so my stitches ended up crooked, but the button, at least, stayed firmly attached.
sentences.zh: 我用針線不算很在行，所以我縫的針腳歪歪扭扭，但至少那顆鈕扣牢牢地固定住了。

sentences.en: William tried to help with the ironing, but Dad gently took the iron away before any actual disaster could happen to his shirts.
sentences.zh: William 想幫忙燙衣服，但爸爸在真正的災難發生在他襯衫上之前，就溫柔地把熨斗收走了。

sentences.en: We found an old craft box full of dried-up glue, half-used paste, and enough loose string to tie up the entire house.
sentences.zh: 我們找到一個舊的美勞箱，裡面全是乾掉的膠水、用了一半的漿糊，還有多到能把整個家都綁起來的散裝細繩。

sentences.en: The bathroom needed fresh soap, extra tissue, and a brand-new toothbrush for William, whose old one had somehow turned an alarming shade of gray.
sentences.zh: 浴室需要補充新的肥皂、多一點衛生紙，還有幫 William 換一支全新的牙刷，他那支舊的不知怎麼變成了令人擔憂的灰色。

sentences.en: Mom mopped the kitchen floor twice, muttering that spring cleaning always reveals exactly how messy the rest of the year really was.
sentences.zh: 媽媽把廚房地板拖了兩次，一邊碎念說春季大掃除總是能揭露這一整年到底有多亂。

sentences.en: We cleaned out the refrigerator together, throwing away three mystery containers that nobody could confidently identify anymore.
sentences.zh: 我們一起清理了冰箱，丟掉了三個沒有人能自信辨認出裡面裝了什麼的神秘容器。

sentences.en: The clothes dryer got a thorough cleaning too, since Dad insisted lint buildup was, quote, "basically a small fire waiting to happen."
sentences.zh: 衣物烘乾機也徹底清理了一遍，因為爸爸堅持棉絮堆積「基本上就是一場等著發生的小火災」。

sentences.en: I finally sorted through my drawer of old pants, an outgrown skirt, a faded T-shirt, and a wool hat I had completely forgotten I owned.
sentences.zh: 我終於整理了我的抽屜，裡面有舊褲子、一件穿不下的裙子、一件褪色的 T 恤，還有一頂我完全忘記自己有的羊毛帽。

sentences.en: We even changed every bed sheet in the house, and Perry immediately claimed the fresh, clean sheets as his personal new napping spot.
sentences.zh: 我們甚至換掉了家裡每一張床單，Perry 馬上就把那些乾淨的新床單當成了他個人的新午睡地點。

sentences.en: By evening, the whole house smelled like soap and fresh laundry, and every piece of furniture sat in a slightly unfamiliar new spot.
sentences.zh: 到了晚上，整間屋子聞起來都是肥皂和剛洗好衣物的味道，每件家具也都坐落在一個稍微陌生的新位置上。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: spring cleaning does not actually make a house bigger, but somehow it always feels like it does, at least for one evening.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：春季大掃除並不會真的讓房子變大，但不知怎麼地，至少在那一個晚上，感覺確實變大了。

quiz.question: What does Sophia ask Coco to do with a needle and thread?
options: Sew a loose button back onto Dad's shirt.
Sew a new mat for the door.

quiz.question: Why does Dad insist on cleaning the clothes dryer thoroughly?
options: He says lint buildup is basically a small fire waiting to happen.

quiz.question: What does Perry do with the freshly changed bed sheets?
options: Claims them as his new napping spot.
Chews a hole in them immediately.
Refuses to go near the bed.
